You are an expert LaTeX-to-Typst converter.

Convert the supplied complete LaTeX document into one complete, standalone Typst
0.14.2 document. Preserve every visible piece of content and the original
document structure as closely as Typst permits. Do not summarize, omit, or
invent content.

Requirements, in priority order:

1. The result must compile with `typst compile` using Typst 0.14.2.
2. Preserve all text, mathematics, headings, lists, tables, figures, captions,
   labels, references, citations, footnotes, and page-level structure.
3. Prefer native semantic Typst constructs such as headings, lists, tables,
   figures, equations, grids, and references. Use manual spacing only when the
   source genuinely requires it.
4. Produce a self-contained document. Do not read local files, use `#include`,
   or depend on images or bibliography files that were not supplied.
5. Use no Typst packages unless conversion is otherwise impossible. Never
   invent package names or versions.
6. Return only raw Typst source. Do not use Markdown fences and do not explain
   the conversion.

Typst syntax checks:

- Put all mathematics inside `$...$`. Typst math is not LaTeX math: do not use
  backslash commands inside Typst math.
- In math, quote ordinary words and separate multi-letter identifiers when
  Typst would otherwise parse them as one variable or a unit.
- Build tables with comma-separated bracketed cells. Do not carry LaTeX `&`,
  `\\`, or column specifications into `table()`.
- `#` enters code from markup. Inside an existing code block, use `let`, `if`,
  and `for` without another `#`.
- Quote paper names, font names, strings, URLs, and other string values.
- A bare `@name` is a Typst reference. Create the matching label before using
  one; escape or otherwise safely represent literal at-signs.
- Enable heading or equation numbering before referencing numbered structures.
- Use valid native functions. When uncertain, choose a simpler native layout
  that compiles instead of guessing an API.

Convert this complete LaTeX document to Typst:

\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{array,booktabs,makecell,multirow,tabularx,longtable,colortbl,xcolor,graphicx}
\usepackage{arydshln}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\title{Simple Tables}
\author{Source-backed grouped table sample}
\date{}
\begin{document}
\maketitle
\section{Tables}

\hline
1  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 1.0 & 8 & tanh & 0.9 & 0.4 & uniform \\
2  & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.1 & 2 & identity & 1.0 & 0.4 & uniform \\

10 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.1 & 10 & tanh & 0.5 & 0.0 & normal \\

22 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.3 & 8 & identity & 0.2 & 0.0 & uniform \\
23 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & tanh & 0.3 & 0.0 & normal \\
24 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.8 & 8 & identity & 0.1 & 0.0 & uniform \\
25 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & relu & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\

}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering

\begin{tabular}[t]{@{}llll@{}}
\hline

#set page(margin: 1in)
#set text(font: "Linux Libertine", size: 11pt)

#align(center, [
  #text(size: 1.5em, weight: "bold")[Simple Tables] \
  Source-backed grouped table sample
])

= Tables
These tables are grouped from source-backed LaTeX table data and compiled as a single benchmark data point.

#figure(
  table(
    columns: 11,
    stroke: (x, y) => if y == 0 or y == 30 { (top: 0.5pt, bottom: 0.5pt) } else { none },
    align: center + horizon,
    [max-min iter.], [$\mu_l$], [$\mu_h$], [$\mu_(x_"in")$], [$\mu_(z_x)$], [$\mu_(r_s)$], [$\mu_c$], [$\mu_a$], [$\mu_(r_"cat")$], [$\mu_m$], [$\mu_d$],
    ..for i in range(1, 30) {
      let data = (
        (5, 10, 3, 5, 1.0, 8, "tanh", 0.9, 0.4, "uniform"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.1, 2, "identity", 1.0, 0.4, "uniform"),
        (12, 128, 13, 200, 0.0, 2, "identity", 0.4, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (3, 5, 2, 6, 0.6, 2, "tanh", 1.0, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (10, 64, 8, 32, 0.4, 2, "tanh", 1.0, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (8, 32, 5, 13, 0.8, 10, "identity", 0.0, 0.2, "normal"),
        (10, 64, 8, 162, 0.9, 6, "elu", 0.6, 0.0, "normal"),
        (5, 10, 3, 13, 0.0, 10, "tanh", 0.8, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (5, 10, 3, 5, 0.3, 6, "identity", 0.6, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (5, 10, 3, 5, 0.1, 10, "tanh", 0.5, 0.0, "normal"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.0, 8, "relu", 1.0, 0.0, "normal"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.6, 4, "relu", 0.6, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.4, 6, "tanh", 0.6, 0.0, "normal"),
        (5, 10, 3, 5, 0.8, 8, "identity", 0.8, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 1.0, 8, "elu", 0.6, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (3, 5, 2, 6, 0.7, 6, "identity", 0.3, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (8, 32, 5, 91, 0.0, 2, "tanh", 1.0, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.0, 8, "tanh", 1.0, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 6, 0.2, 6, "tanh", 0.2, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (12, 128, 13, 200, 0.2, 2, "identity", 0.1, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 6, 0.9, 8, "relu", 0.6, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.3, 8, "identity", 0.2, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (5, 10, 3, 5, 0.2, 4, "tanh", 0.3, 0.0, "normal"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.8, 8, "identity", 0.1, 0.0, "uniform"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.2, 4, "relu", 1.0, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.5, 8, "tanh", 0.8, 0.0, "normal"),
        (8, 32, 5, 13, 0.1, 4, "identity", 0.0, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 6, 0.4, 4, "tanh", 0.1, 0.0, "exponential"),
        (3, 5, 2, 5, 0.1, 8, "identity", 0.2, 0.0, "exponential")
      )
      let row = data.at(i - 1)
      (str(i), ..row.map(str))
    }
  ),
  caption: [Source table 1: 2512.03307_table_5]
)

#figure(
  table(
    columns: 3,
    stroke: (x, y) => if y == 0 or y == 36 { (top: 0.5pt, bottom: 0.5pt) } else { none },
    align: (left, left, left),
    [#], [Word type], [$f$],
    [1.], [_te_ (‘at’, ‘to’, #smallcaps[comp])], [1018],
    [2.], [_dat_ (‘that’)], [658],

    [14.], [_een_ (‘a’, ‘one’)], [212],
    [15.], [_ik_ (‘I’)], [194],
    [16.], [_massa's_ (‘masses’)], [186],
    [17.], [_het_ (‘the’, #smallcaps[neut:sg])], [180],

    [19.], [_deze_ (‘this’, #smallcaps[masc/fem])], [172],

    [22.], [_aan_ (‘to’, #smallcaps[prep])], [161],
    [23.], [_veel_ (‘a lot of’)], [155],
    [24.], [_hier_ (‘here’)], [146],
    [25.], [_voor_ (‘for’)], [144],
    [26.], [_dit_ (‘this’, #smallcaps[neut])], [140],

    [30.], [_menig_ (‘many’, #smallcaps[sg])], [118],

)
